title: mentha × verticillata var. origanifolia
intro: Mentha × verticillata var. origanifolia, also known as the mint-like or peppermint-leaved mint, is a hybrid of the peppermint (Mentha × piperita) and water mint (Mentha aquatica), characterized by its distinctive, fragrant leaves and stems. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to ease digestive issues and reduce inflammation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The health benefits of Mentha × verticillata var. origanifolia include its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), reduce nausea and headaches, and even help with weight loss. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, relieve stress, and improve overall well-being.
